# Architectural Blueprint Description

This aged architectural drawing on beige paper displays a detailed longitudinal section view of a large Victorian-era building. The main elevation runs horizontally across the lower portion of the page, showing a complex multi-story structure with distinctive features including a tall conical tower on the left side, multiple pitched roofs with ventilation cupolas, and numerous windows and doors arranged across multiple levels. The building features ornate details including railings, columns, and decorative elements typical of 19th-century design. Below the main section drawing, text labels identify different areas including "Main Part," "Inner Porch," "Servants Hall," "Scullery," and "Veranda" with measurements and scale notations. In the upper right portion of the page, a smaller transverse section drawing shows the building's cross-section with internal structural details including roof trusses and floor levels. Additional handwritten notes appear at the top of the page. Fine pencil lines with brown ink detailing show the precise architectural measurements and construction specifications throughout both views. A colored scale bar appears on the right edge of the document.

# Architectural Blueprint Description

This is a detailed second floor plan drawn on aged, cream-colored paper with visible stains and wear marks. At the top of the page is a color reference scale with various colored bars (blue, cyan, green, yellow, magenta, pink, white, and dark gray) alongside a measurement ruler. The main floor plan features architectural drawings in brown and black ink with a scale notation of "Eight Feet to One Inch" printed at the bottom left. The plan shows multiple rooms with labeled dimensions, doorways, and staircases. Notable labeled spaces include "Bedroom" rooms marked with measurements like "13'6" x 16'6"", "Bath Room" areas with dimensions such as "11'6" x 14'", and various "Bed Room" spaces numbered (3) and (4). A central staircase is depicted with cross-hatching. The bottom of the document reads "SECOND FLOOR" in capital letters, and the credit line states "F. McLINUS & SON, ARCHTS.--" with an address "530 PINE ST, S.F." The entire document appears aged and fragile with various brown discoloration marks distributed across the surface.

# Architectural Floor Plan Description

This is a historical architectural blueprint showing the first floor plan of a building, drawn by E. M. Dougall & Son, Architects, located at 530 Pine Street, San Francisco. The document is printed on aged, cream-colored paper with visible stains, creases, and deterioration marks typical of early architectural drawings.

The floor plan displays a symmetrical, multi-section layout with three distinct horizontal levels of rooms. The upper section contains a central kitchen with adjacent pantries, serveries, and smaller utility rooms, along with staircases. The middle section features two large mirrored rooms labeled "Old Ladies Dining Room" and "Children's Dining Room," each measuring 30' x 30', with flanking smaller rooms including a "Linen Closet" and "Children's Entrance" in the center. The lower section shows an extended suite of rooms including a "Hall Vestibule," "Sitting Rooms," "Parlor," and multiple "Bed Rooms" of varying sizes, with dimensions noted throughout (such as 13' x 15', 14' x 13', and 12' x 13').

A measurement scale reading "Eight Feet to One Inch" appears at the bottom of the plan. A color reference bar is visible at the top of the page. All rooms are labeled with handwritten or printed text, and dimension lines with measurements are drawn throughout the plan. The drawing style uses ink lines on the aged paper with some brown staining visible across the document.
